steps.title: Link growth to the maps
steps.content: The student needs to connect Morris's quotation about maps of Manchester to the UK's urban population growth during the Industrial Revolution. The notes say UK urban population grew substantially, Manchester grew especially sharply, and before-and-after maps reflect Manchester's expansion. The correct sentence must keep all three pieces connected.

steps.title: Keep the statistics accurate
steps.content: A assigns Manchester's 2,000 percent increase to the UK overall. B makes relocation itself the visual representation, even though Morris describes maps. C changes the maps from Manchester to the UK and shifts the focus to the rural landscape. The accurate choice links UK urban growth, Manchester maps, and Morris's quotation. The answer is D.
